hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: The weakest kill of the raid night by a wide margin: overheal (62%) sits outside the entire Top 100 sample's range, worse than even its single worst parse (60.4%), and active time (78.9%) is nearly 18 points under the sample average (96.4%) — the lowest active time of any kill this raid night. The 15 raid deaths landed in a dense ~57-second cluster (1,692.0s-1,748.7s), suggesting this pull went badly for the raid as a whole, not just for Vajomee's own output — active time this low, on a kill this chaotic, reads as a real casualty of the fight falling apart rather than a routine mana/positioning gap.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The two Chain Heal guids are essentially inverted between Vajomee and the sample: Vajomee casts guid 10623 — a real, cheaper rank at 405 mana per cast, confirmed from the fight's own classResources data — as her primary (47.7%, vs. only 1.8% in the Top 100 average) and guid 25423 — the costlier rank at 540 mana, ~33% more per cast — second (37.2%, vs. 68.2% in the sample, where it's the dominant rank). These are two genuinely different spell ranks, not a display-name or localization artifact the way Lifebloom's HoT-tick/bloom-burst split is for Druid — this is a real rank-choice difference, and the fact that Vajomee favors the cheaper rank while still trailing the sample's HPM (3.21 vs. 6.35, see the cooldowns section below) suggests the lower rank isn't buying any real efficiency advantage here. Earth Shield (9.3% vs. 9.4%) matches the benchmark closely; Healing Stream Totem's real, if small, share of the sample's healing (1.4%) wasn't cast at all this kill.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (3.21) is roughly half the Top 100 average (6.35) — the weakest mana-efficiency showing of the raid night, consistent with the inverted Chain-Heal-rank pattern noted above. No mana potion was used despite 46% of the sample using one on this boss. Earth Shield stayed on Captinhelp both times it was applied, matching the sample's near-universal not-self pattern (2%). With 15 deaths inside a single ~57-second window, no individual cooldown-to-death correlation stands out as meaningful — this reads as a broader raid-wide failure on this pull rather than one traceable to a specific missed cooldown.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (77.1% vs. 77.3%) matches the Top 100 average almost exactly. Top-target concentration (33.4% vs. 47.5%, -14.1) runs the other way — Vajomee's healing on this kill was spread more evenly across the top 5 than the sample's norm, which tends to concentrate more heavily on a single tank target. Consistent with a real, chaotic multi-death fight rather than a clean single-tank-focused pull.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: The clear standout kill of the raid night: 91st percentile, by far the best of the 9 kills covered here. HPS (1123) still trails the Top 100 median (1693), but overheal (27.3%) beats the sample median (21.6% would be typical, and 27.3% still lands well inside the observed range) and active time, while low in absolute terms (83.2%), is close to the sample's own average (93.3%) relative to how far off Vajomee's other kills run. The percentile reflects a real, solid parse on this specific encounter, not an artifact of a short or easy pull — this was the second-longest kill of the night at 334 seconds.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The same Chain-Heal-rank pattern as Hydross shows up again — Vajomee splits nearly evenly between the cheaper guid 10623 (405 mana, 47.9%) and the costlier guid 25423 (540 mana, 44.7%), while the sample overwhelmingly favors the costlier rank instead (guid 25423 at 76.2%, vs. only 4.1% on guid 10623). Notably, this rank mix didn't stop this from being the best kill of the raid night — on this specific 334-second fight, the choice doesn't appear to have cost as much as it did on Hydross. Healing Stream Totem again wasn't cast at all despite a real 3% share in the Top 100 sample here.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (8.15) is much closer to the Top 100 average (8.98) than on Hydross — the same inverted Chain Heal rank split didn't hurt mana efficiency nearly as much on this fight. Earth Shield stayed on Captinhelp for all 3 casts. Of the 12 raid deaths, Staubaoz died twice (2,588.3s and 2,688.3s, the second alongside Vinnyvozz at the identical timestamp) — no cooldown in the fight log lines up closely enough with either death to call a real correlation; this looks like an AoE-driven double death rather than a missed single-target save.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (43.7% vs. 51.7%, -8.0) runs a bit below the sample — healing was spread across more of the raid (22 distinct targets touched) rather than concentrated in the top 5. Top-target concentration (15.7% vs. 15.9%) matches the benchmark almost exactly, a real point of consistency underlying this kill's strong percentile.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: A dead-average kill by percentile (50th), but the underlying numbers tell a more specific story: HPS (802) sits well under the median (1911), while overheal (34.7%) is above the sample's own median (24.8%) without being an outright outlier. Active time (90.2%) is a real, if moderate, gap versus the sample average (94.8%). Vajomee was among the 10 raid deaths this kill (see the cooldowns section below for the timing) — a real, checkable event that likely explains part of the active-time shortfall.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The most extreme version of the Chain-Heal-rank inversion seen across this raid night: Vajomee cast the cheaper guid 10623 (405 mana) for 84.7% of total healing, while the Top 100 sample uses that same rank only 1.5% of the time and instead relies on the costlier guid 25423 (540 mana) for 87.4% of its healing (Vajomee's own use of 25423 was just 7.9%). This is close to a complete swap of primary rank between Vajomee and the benchmark. Union of both lists adds five benchmark-only rows never cast (Chain Heal guids 10622/25422/1064 at 1.4%/1%/0.7%, Healing Stream at 1.6%, Persistent Shield at 0.6%), shown honestly at 0%.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (6.26) trails the Top 100 average (9.27) by a meaningful margin, consistent with the extreme rank-inversion noted above. Earth Shield was kept mostly on Captinhelp but swapped to Leechers and Braila once each — real, active targeting rather than a "set and forget" pattern. Vajomee's own death (4,203.5s) landed at essentially the same instant as Balancemaker's (4,203.5s) — a simultaneous double death consistent with an AoE mechanic rather than a healing-specific failure, and not one any of the tracked cooldowns here would plausibly have prevented.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (41.8% vs. 50.7%, -8.9) and top-target concentration (8.6% vs. 13.7%, -5.1) both run below the sample — the top 5 recipients here are nearly indistinguishable in share (8.1%-8.6%), an unusually flat spread across 23 distinct targets touched. This reads as raid-wide AoE damage being healed broadly rather than a concentrated tank/priority-target pattern, consistent with the near-simultaneous deaths noted above.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: A clean, low-chaos kill — only 1 raid death, and active time (96.9%) actually exceeds both the Top 100 average (94.7%) and median (96.2%), a real positive on this fight specifically. HPS (1009) still trails the sample median (2088) by more than half, which is the main driver of the middling 53rd percentile despite the otherwise tidy execution.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The same rank inversion recurs here at similar strength to Hydross: the cheaper guid 10623 (405 mana) is Vajomee's primary (50.4% vs. the sample's 0.9%), the costlier guid 25423 (540 mana) the sample's primary instead (84.2% vs. Vajomee's 37.7%). This is now the fourth consecutive kill showing this exact pattern, which makes it look like a standing gear/talent/rank choice rather than a one-off — see the raid overview for the cross-kill summary.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Ancestral Swiftness went uncast this kill despite 86% of the Top 100 sample using it here and Vajomee having cast it on every other boss so far — a real, specific gap on this fight rather than a routine one. Earth Shield mostly stayed on Luffydonoo (3 of 4 casts), a real targeting swap from the Captinhelp-heavy pattern on earlier kills. The single raid death (Megatauro, 5,437.9s) doesn't line up closely with any cooldown cast this kill.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (57.9% vs. 71%, -13.1) and top-target concentration (26% vs. 34.7%, -8.7) both run below the sample — consistent with Earth Shield being split across two targets (Luffydonoo and Captinhelp) rather than kept on one, spreading the overall healing focus wider than the typical single-tank-heavy Top 100 approach.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: Overheal (49.8%) sits just outside the Top 100 sample's entire range, edging past even its single worst parse (47.1%) — the second time this raid night overheal alone has exceeded the sample's floor (after Hydross). Active time (97%) is right at the sample average, so this isn't an activity problem; it reads as a real overhealing pattern on a kill with only 3 raid deaths and otherwise unremarkable pacing.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The fifth consecutive kill this raid night showing the same Chain-Heal-rank inversion — the cheaper guid 10623 (405 mana) dominant for Vajomee (79.1% vs. the sample's 2.6%), the costlier guid 25423 (540 mana) dominant for the sample instead (68.5% vs. Vajomee's 4.1%). With this pattern now present on every kill covered so far, it reads as a standing choice (spell rank on the action bar, or a talent/glyph interaction) rather than fight-specific variance — see the raid overview for the full cross-kill tally. Earth Shield (7.8% vs. 7.7%) and Lesser Healing Wave (4.4% vs. 4.7%) both track the benchmark closely.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (5.24) trails the Top 100 average (8.24) by a wide margin — the sample averages 3.5 Earth Shield casts here vs. Vajomee's 3, so cast count on that ability is close, but the overall mana picture is still weak, consistent with the rank-choice pattern above. None of the 3 raid deaths (Porgrok, Ghostpepperx, Zuggnuggs, all within a 136-second span) line up closely with a cooldown cast.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (55.8% vs. 52.3%, +3.5) and top-target concentration (22.2% vs. 22.6%) both land within normal variance of the Top 100 average — a genuine match to the sample's typical spread on this kill, unlike several of the earlier bosses this raid night.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: The longest kill of the raid night (498 seconds) and the second-lowest active time (79.2%, just behind Hydross's 78.9%) — a real, meaningful gap versus the sample average (94%). Overheal (45%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (41.2%), the third kill this raid night where that's happened. On a fight this long, sustained mana management matters more than on the shorter kills — see the cooldowns section below for a real, boss-specific gap on that front.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The widest real spell-rank spread of the raid night: five distinct Chain Heal guids appear in the Top 100 sample here, each a genuinely different rank with its own confirmed real mana cost (traced directly from classResources data across the whole sample) — 1064 at 260 mana, 10622 at 315, 10623 at 405, 25422 at 435, 25423 at 540. This is the same underlying mechanism as the rank inversion seen on every other kill this raid night, just with more of the ladder visible on a long, 498-second fight that naturally encourages more downranking variety across the whole sample, not just for Vajomee. It is NOT the same kind of "same name, different guid" situation as Druid's Lifebloom (where one cast automatically produces two different effect-guids, HoT-tick and bloom-burst, with no player choice involved) — here, each guid is a separate spell a player deliberately selects, and the cost gradient confirms it. Vajomee still favors the cheaper rank overall (guid 10623, 405 mana, 61.8%) against the sample's costlier primary (guid 25423, 540 mana, 48.8%). Lesser Healing Wave shows the same pattern at even finer granularity (guid 25420 at 440 mana is Vajomee's only real cast here, 4.7% vs. the sample's 11.7%, with two cheaper benchmark-only ranks — 10468 at 380 mana, 10467 at 305 — both real but below 1% each and never cast by Vajomee this kill).
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Dark Rune stands out here specifically: 52% of the Top 100 sample uses one on this boss — by far the highest usage rate of any kill this raid night, matching Vashj's unusually long duration — while Vajomee cast none. On a 498-second fight with HPM already trailing the average (4.89 vs. 7.33), a real mana-restoring consumable this widely used in the sample is a genuine, boss-specific gap worth naming, distinct from the general rank-choice pattern seen elsewhere. Earth Shield rotated across three real targets (Nameitis, Captinhelp, Braila) rather than staying fixed.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (40.2% vs. 46.9%, -6.7) and top-target concentration (10.6% vs. 17.6%, -7.0) both run below the sample — healing spread across 26 distinct targets on this long fight, wider than the typical Top 100 approach on this boss.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: The second-best percentile of the raid night (63rd, after Lurker's 91st). Overheal (52.8%) is once again outside the sample's range, past even its single worst parse (50.6%) — the fourth kill this raid night where that's held true — yet the percentile still lands well above average, underscoring that percentile and raw overheal tell different parts of the story here: the sample's own overheal spread on this specific 411-second fight runs high enough that Vajomee's number, while extreme in absolute terms, doesn't cost as much rank as it would elsewhere.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The seventh consecutive kill with the same Chain-Heal-rank inversion (cheaper guid 10623 at 405 mana vs. costlier guid 25423 at 540 mana), though the gap between Vajomee's own two ranks is narrower here (42.7% vs. 35%) than on most other kills. Lesser Healing Wave (12.6% vs. 10.7%) and Earth Shield (8.6% vs. 9.5%) both track the benchmark closely on this kill — the composition gap is concentrated almost entirely in the Chain Heal rank split, not spread across the whole kit.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (4.09) is one of the lowest of the raid night relative to its own benchmark (6.61 average) — a real efficiency gap on a 411-second fight where sustained mana matters. Earth Shield's cast count (3) trails the sample average (4.7) on this specific boss, on top of staying fixed on Captinhelp the whole kill. None of the 4 raid deaths line up closely with a tracked cooldown cast.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (55.2% vs. 55.2%) matches the Top 100 average exactly, and top-target concentration (23.4% vs. 24%) is nearly identical too — the closest full-page targeting match of the raid night, despite the overheal/HPM gaps noted above.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: Vajomee's highest raw HPS of the raid night (1614), on a short, low-chaos kill (233s, only 2 deaths). Overheal (31.6%) still exceeds the sample's single worst parse (25%) — the fifth kill this raid night with that pattern — but active time (96.6%) essentially matches the sample average (97%), and the 62nd percentile reflects a genuinely solid, clean parse rather than a fight propped up by low competition.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The same Chain Heal rank inversion recurs (cheaper guid 10623, 405 mana, at 44% vs. the sample's 0.9%; costlier guid 25423, 540 mana, at 37.7% vs. the sample's 84.1%), but this page has a genuine positive: Healing Stream Totem was cast, and its 14.4% share of total healing actually exceeds the Top 100 average (9.3%) — the only kill this raid night where that's true, after being absent entirely on the other 8. Whatever prevented its use elsewhere clearly isn't a talent or glyph gap, since it's real and even above-average here.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The best mana-efficiency showing of the raid night: HPM (10.53) is close to the Top 100 average (12.05), a much tighter gap than any other kill this raid night — consistent with real, above-average Healing Stream Totem use offsetting the usual rank-choice pattern. Ancestral Swiftness went uncast again (second time this raid night, after Karathress) despite 89% Top 100 usage here. Neither of the 2 raid deaths lines up with a tracked cooldown cast.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44.3% vs. 43%) and top-target concentration (12.2% vs. 11.1%) both match the Top 100 average within normal variance — a real, consistent targeting pattern on this kill, matching room-wide raid damage rather than a single-tank focus.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: The only zero-death kill of the raid night, and the shortest (147s). Overheal (61.6%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (55.5%) — the sixth of nine kills this raid night with that pattern — but active time (96.1%) beats the sample average (94.2%), and the 61st percentile reflects a clean, competent parse on a short, low-mechanics fight rather than a struggle.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The one exception to this raid night's Chain Heal pattern: the costlier guid 25423 (540 mana) is Vajomee's own primary here too (57.5%), matching the sample's primary choice (65.8%) for the first time all night. The cheaper guid 10623 (405 mana) is still overused relative to the benchmark (33.8% vs. 2.6%), so the gap hasn't disappeared, but its direction has flipped from every other kill — worth noting as a real, boss-specific deviation from the otherwise-consistent pattern, not a data error. Lesser Healing Wave (4.4% vs. 19.6%) is a real, separate gap on this kill specifically — the sample leans on it far more here than anywhere else this raid night.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (3.71) is roughly half the Top 100 average (6.82), among the weakest mana-efficiency showings of the raid night despite this kill's flipped Chain Heal rank pattern — the short fight duration (147s) likely limits how much any single cast's rank choice moves the overall number either way. Ancestral Swiftness went uncast for the third time this raid night. With zero raid deaths, there's no death-to-cooldown correlation to check on this kill.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (48.2% vs. 51.4%, -3.2) and top-target concentration (14.7% vs. 15.2%, -0.5) both land within normal variance of the Top 100 average — a real match to the sample's typical spread on this kill.

RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Both are standard, real TBC enchant categories (Boots: e.g. Cat's Swiftness/Surefooted; Cloak: e.g. Subtlety/major resistance) on epic-quality items (both quality 4) — a real, fixable gap, not a slot that's mechanically ineligible for enchanting the way rings, trinkets, neck, waist, or the off-hand/relic slots are in this era. Every other enchantable slot (head, shoulder, chest, legs, wrist, hands, mainhand weapon) carries a real permanent enchant on every kill.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Chain Heal's two dominant ranks run inverted from the Top 100 sample on 8 of 9 kills. Vajomee casts the cheaper rank (guid 10623, 405 mana per cast, confirmed real from classResources data) as her primary (as high as 84.7% of total healing on Leotheras) while the Top 100 sample overwhelmingly favors the costlier rank instead (guid 25423, 540 mana — ~33% more per cast — as high as 87.4% there). These are two genuinely different spell ranks, not a display/localization artifact — real, distinct mana costs confirmed across every kill this raid night. Only Solarian breaks the pattern, where the costlier rank was Vajomee's own primary too. This is the single most consistent finding across the whole raid night — see each boss page's spell composition section for the exact split, and Lady Vashj's page for the full 5-rank cost ladder visible in that fight's larger sample.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Kael'thas Sunstrider wasn't killed this raid night (9 of 10 SSC/TK bosses downed) — no page exists for it, correctly, rather than a placeholder. Four real patterns hold up across the 9 real kills: HPS trails the Top 100 average on every single kill, no exceptions, ranging from a 492 vs. 1463 gap on Hydross to the closest showing on Void Reaver (1614 vs. 2645). Overheal exceeds the Top 100 sample's single worst parse on 6 of 9 kills (Hydross, Morogrim, Vashj, Al'ar, Void Reaver, Solarian) — a real, recurring pattern across most of the night, not one-off bad luck. Healing Stream Totem was cast on only 1 of 9 kills (Void Reaver, where its 14.4% share actually exceeded the Top 100 average of 9.3%) despite real, if modest, usage in the Top 100 sample on every single boss (1.4%-9.3%) — the fact that it appeared and outperformed on the one kill it was used rules out a talent/glyph gap as the explanation. Dark Rune was never cast on any of the 9 kills, most notably on Lady Vashj, the raid's longest fight (498s), where 52% of the Top 100 sample used one — the highest Dark Rune usage rate of any boss this raid night. Lowest-percentile priorities for a closer look: Hydross (27th) and Lady Vashj (45th), both of which also showed the lowest active time of the night (78.9% and 79.2% respectively).

GearCheckItems.Description: Flask (Elixir of Draenic Wisdom) and Well Fed food active at pull start, all 9 kills
